market_context: SPY and QQQ closed Friday at 52-week highs (+6.3% and +8.2% above 20MA respectively). Monday pre-market is materially different from the stale Friday input data: a U.S.-Iran military confrontation (U.S. Navy seized Iranian cargo ship; Iran vowing retaliation) is driving Nasdaq futures down ~1% and crude oil prices sharply higher on Strait of Hormuz supply-disruption fears. Energy sector outperforming; tech/growth facing headwinds. Bias is sector-split: energy bullish on oil surge, broad tech bearish on geopolitical risk-off.

trades.context: U.S.-Iran military confrontation over the weekend (U.S. Navy seized Iranian cargo ship; Iran vowing retaliation) drove crude oil sharply higher on Strait of Hormuz supply-disruption fears. XOM is up ~1.75% pre-market at approximately $146.26. Despite being in a technical downtrend (below its 20MA and 50MA), this is a pure catalyst-driven day trade on energy momentum — geopolitical oil shocks can produce rapid 3-5% intraday moves in integrated majors.
if_conditions: XOM holds above $145.00 at market open (pre-market support level; break below signals oil pop is fading)
No ceasefire or de-escalation announcement from Iran within the first 15 minutes of trading
SPY is not declining more than -1.5% at 9:35am ET (broad market crash would overwhelm the energy bid)

trade.stop: -35% on premium OR XOM price drops below $144.50

trades.invalidation: If XOM opens below $144.50 or immediately reverses lower at the open, skip entirely — the oil pop is fading and the catalyst is not holding.

trades.context: QQQ closed Friday at a 52-week high ($646.89, range_position_pct=100%), leaving it maximally exposed to a pullback. Monday pre-market Nasdaq futures are explicitly lower on Iran-driven geopolitical shock. Buying puts at an all-time high with a confirmed negative catalyst and cheap implied volatility (HV30=22%) is a high-quality asymmetric setup — maximum technical vulnerability plus a specific negative event.
if_conditions: QQQ opens below $643.00 (below Friday's close of $646.89, confirming the Iran-driven gap-down is holding at open)
SPY is declining at least -0.5% from its own open at 9:35am ET (confirming broad risk-off, not isolated sector rotation)
No sudden Iran de-escalation or ceasefire headline within the first 15 minutes of trading

trade.stop: -35% on premium OR QQQ price recovers above $646.00 (above Friday's close)

trades.invalidation: If QQQ opens above $645 or immediately rallies from the open (Iran fears dissipate on ceasefire news), skip entirely.

skipped: TSLA — earnings Apr 22 (within 2 days, mandatory skip per rules)

NFLX — post-earnings guidance miss (guided Q2 and FY26 below consensus, stock -8.9% AH); however, earnings shock already absorbed into Friday's close at $97.99 and post-earnings IV remains elevated vs. HV30=37%, creating IV crush risk on new put positions
AMD — strong breakout setup (range_position_pct=98.5%, near 52-week high) but Nasdaq futures down on Iran = poor market alignment for bullish tech
AMZN — near 52-week high (range 95.4%), strong trend, but Nasdaq facing Iran-driven headwinds today
AVGO — near 52-week high (range 96.2%), strong trend; HV30=44.4% elevated and Nasdaq headwinds disqualify
CVX — same oil/Iran catalyst as XOM but weaker pre-market move (+1.07% vs +1.75%); XOM is the cleaner expression of the same trade
MSTR — down 7.01% pre-market but HV30=62.9%; puts too expensive with no specific catalyst beyond crypto risk-off
MS — strong trend near 52-week high (+0.83% pre-market) but no specific catalyst to justify entry in a risk-off environment
